title: chromolaena pulchella
intro: Chromolaena pulchella, also known as Mexican marigold or Lantana of the Andes, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Chromolaena pulchella include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce stress. Chromolaena pulchella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
